page: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-3xL4 is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. The hardware configuration features 20 vCPUs with a shared CPU allocation model and 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding 12.8 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is provided by three NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace family, delivering a combined 72 GB of VRAM.

This server type does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached volumes. It includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. A key architectural tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which can lead to variable CPU performance depending on host node conditions, though this is offset by the dedicated GPU resources and large system memory pool.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance provides a high density of GPU accelerators and system memory relative to the vCPU count, making it a specialized option for workloads that rely primarily on GPU compute rather than dedicated CPU cycles. Suitable workloads include machine learning inference, parallel data processing, video transcoding, and 3D rendering pipelines where the 72 GB of aggregate VRAM and 256 GB of system RAM can be fully utilized.
description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-3xL4 is an x86_64 GPU-accelerated server configured with 20 shared vCPUs, 256.0 GB of system memory, and three NVIDIA L4 GPUs from the Ada Lovelace architecture, providing 72 GB of total VRAM. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this instance allocates 12.8 GB of RAM per core but lacks local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions. The shared CPU allocation presents a performance tradeoff for CPU-bound tasks, but the high GPU-to-vCPU density and large memory capacity offer qualitative cost efficiency for GPU-centric workloads. This server is designed for workloads such as machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and data science tasks that require substantial VRAM and system memory without needing dedicated, non-shared CPU resources.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-3xL4 is an x86_64 KVM server featuring 20 shared vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA L4 GPUs with 72 GB VRAM. It lacks local storage and is designed for GPU-heavy workloads.

tagline: A GPU-accelerated server featuring three NVIDIA L4 GPUs, 256 GB of system memory, and shared x86_64 vCPUs for parallel processing workloads.

20 vCPUs utilizing a shared CPU allocation model
256.0 GB of system memory providing 12.8 GB per core
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage
